Amazon.com is sued over alleged sale of contaminated rice Amazon.com was sued on Friday by consumers over its alleged sale of a wide variety of rice contaminated by arsenic and other heavy metals.  The proposed class action in Seattle federal court covers 18 types of rice sold through Amazon, including from familiar brands such as Bens Original and Amazon-owned Whole Foods 365.  Amazon sold these rice products with alarmingly high levels of heavy metals to an intended consumer audience that includes children, with no warning whatsoever about the dangers of heavy metals, the complaint said.
